# cloudflare_origin_cloud_region (Resource)



## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "cloudflare_origin_cloud_region" "example_origin_cloud_region" {
  zone_id = "023e105f4ecef8ad9ca31a8372d0c353"
  origin_ip = "192.0.2.1"
  region = "us-east-1"
  vendor = "aws"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `origin_ip` (String) Origin IP address (IPv4 or IPv6). For the single PUT endpoint (`PUT /origin/cloud_regions/{origin_ip}`), this field must match the path parameter or the request will be rejected with a 400 error. For the batch PUT endpoint, this field identifies which mapping to upsert.
- `region` (String) Cloud vendor region identifier. Must be a valid region for the specified vendor as returned by the supported_regions endpoint.
- `vendor` (String) Cloud vendor hosting the origin. Must be one of the supported vendors.
Available values: "aws", "azure", "gcp", "oci".
- `zone_id` (String) Identifier.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Origin IP address (IPv4 or IPv6). For the single PUT endpoint (`PUT /origin/cloud_regions/{origin_ip}`), this field must match the path parameter or the request will be rejected with a 400 error. For the batch PUT endpoint, this field identifies which mapping to upsert.
- `modified_on` (String) Time this mapping was last modified.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
$ terraform import cloudflare_origin_cloud_region.example '<zone_id>/<origin_ip>'
```
